title: Storage control system comprising a supercontinuum laser source
abstract: A storage control system (10) for controlling a storage space parameter of a storage space (100) for produce (110), the storage control system (10) comprising a (i) controller (300) and (ii) a gas sensor system (200) configured to analyze a storage space gas sample, wherein the gas sensor system (200) comprises (a) a light source (210) comprising a supercontinuum laser source, (b) a water trap (240) configured for continuously removing water vapor from gas passing through the water trap (240), (c) a gas cell (220) for part of the storage space gas sample, and (d) a sensor (230), wherein the storage control system (10) is configured to provide the storage space gas sample from the storage space (100) to the gas cell (220) via the water trap (240), wherein the light source (210) is configured to provide light source radiation (211) to the gas cell (220), wherein the sensor (230) is configured to sense radiation (221) emitted from the gas cell (220) and to provide a corresponding sensor signal (235) to the controller (300), wherein the sensor signal (235) comprises absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236), wherein the controller (300) is configured to execute a data processing operation to provide a processed sensor signal (237), wherein the data processing operation comprises aligning of the absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236) relative to one or more reference wavelength-dependent data (238), wherein the controller (300) is configured to control the storage space parameter dependent on the processed sensor signal (237).
claims:
Claims 1. A storage control system (10) for controlling a storage space parameter of a storage space (100) for produce (1 10), the storage control system (10) comprising a (i) controller (300) and (ii) a gas sensor system (200) configured to analyze a storage space gas sample, wherein the gas sensor system (200) comprises (a) a light source (210) comprising a supercontinuum laser source, (b) a water trap (240) configured for continuously removing water vapor from gas passing through the water trap (240), (c) a gas cell (220) for part of the storage space gas sample, and (d) a sensor (230), wherein the storage control system (10) is configured to provide the storage space gas sample from the storage space (100) to the gas cell (220) via the water trap (240), wherein the light source (210) is configured to provide light source radiation (21 1) to the gas cell (220), wherein the sensor (230) is configured to sense radiation (221) emitted from the gas cell (220) and to provide a corresponding sensor signal (235) to the controller (300), wherein the sensor signal (235) comprises absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236), wherein the controller (300) is configured to execute a data processing operation to provide a processed sensor signal (237), wherein the data processing operation comprises aligning of the absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236) relative to one or more reference wavelength-dependent data (238), wherein the controller (300) is configured to control the storage space parameter dependent on the processed sensor signal (237). 2. The storage control system (10) according to claim 1, wherein the controller (300) is configured to control the storage space parameter dependent on a predetermined relation between the storage space parameter and the processed sensor signal (237). 3. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the gas sensor system (200) is configured to sense a gaseous compound (120), and wherein the sensor signal (235) comprises concentration-related information in relation to a gaseous compound (120) in the storage space gas (130). 4. The storage control system (10) according to claim 3, wherein the gas sensor system (200) is configured to sense a gaseous compound (120) selected from the group consisting of ethanol, acetaldehyde, ethylacetate, methanol, acetone, ethylene, C0 2 , O2, and ethane. 5. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the storage space parameter comprises one or more of a CO2 concentration, an 0 2 concentration, an N 2 concentration, an inert gas concentration, an ethylene concentration, a temperature, a pressure, a gas flow and a humidity of the storage space gas (130), a storage time, and an illumination of the storage space (100). 6. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the storage control system (10) is configured to control one or more of spoilage, rotting, fermentation, respiration, and ripening of the produce by controlling the storage space parameter. 7. The storage control system (10) according to claims 5-6, wherein the storage space parameter comprises at least the ethylene concentration, and wherein the controller (300) is configured to control ripening by controlling the ethylene concentration. 8. The storage control system (10) according to claims 5-6 or claim 7, wherein the storage space parameter comprises at least the 0 2 concentration and the C0 2 concentration, and wherein the controller (300) is configured to control respiration by controlling the 0 2 concentration and the C0 2 concentration. 9. The storage control system (10) according to claims 5-6 or any one of claims 7-8, wherein the storage space parameter comprises at least the 0 2 concentration, and wherein the controller (300) is configured to control fermentation by controlling the 0 2 concentration. 10. The storage control system (10) according to claims 5-6 or any one of claims 7-9, wherein the storage space parameter comprises at least the storage time, and wherein the controller (300) is configured to control rotting by controlling the storage time. 11. The storage control system (10) according to claims 5-6 or any one of claims 7-10, wherein the storage space parameter comprises at least the storage time, and wherein the controller (300) is configured to control spoilage by controlling the storage time. 12. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the light source radiation (21 1) comprises supercontinuum laser light having wavelengths in a range of 2.0 to 20 pm. 13. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the gas cell (220) comprises a multipass gas cell, wherein an optical path for the light source radiation in the multipass gas cell is at least 1 m. 14. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sensor (230) comprises (i) a converter, configured to convert at least part of the radiation emitted from the gas cell (220) into converted radiation, wherein the converter comprises a photon upconverter or a photon downconverter, and (ii) a detector, configured to sense the converted radiation and to provide the corresponding sensor signal (235) to the controller (300). 15. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the water trap (240) comprises a plurality of water trap chambers (245), wherein each of the water trap chambers (245) comprises or is functionally coupled to a temperature control unit, wherein the temperature control unit is configured to control the temperature of the respective water trap chamber (245), wherein the controller (300) is configured to independently operate each of the water trap chambers (245) alternatingly in a water capture mode and in an ice cleansing mode, wherein: 

 - the water capture mode comprises controlling the temperature of the respective water trap chamber (245) at or below a water freezing temperature; - the ice cleansing mode comprises controlling the temperature of the respective water trap chamber (245) at or above a water melting temperature; wherein during operation of the storage control system at least one of the water trap chambers (245) is in the water capture mode. 16. The storage control system (10) according to claim 15, wherein the temperature control unit comprises a Peltier element comprising a second stage Peltier cooling layer applied to a first Peltier cooling layer. 17. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the gas cell (220) comprises mirrors, wherein the mirrors (220) comprise a material having a minimal reflectivity of 99.2% averaged over the 2-20 pm wavelength range. 18. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the gas cell (220) is functionally coupled to a wavelength dependent optical filter arranged upstream of the gas cell, wherein the wavelength dependent optical filter is configured to filter out one or more wavelengths from the light source radiation (211). 19. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the gas sensor system (200) comprises a beam splitter (260) arranged upstream of the gas cell (220), wherein the beam splitter (260) is configured to split off part of the light source radiation (211) into reference radiation (261), wherein the reference radiation (261) bypasses the gas cell (220), wherein the sensor (230) is configured to sense the reference radiation (261) and to provide a reference signal to the controller (300), wherein the controller (300) is configured to normalize the sensor signal (235) based on the sensed reference radiation (261). 20. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the reference wavelength-dependent data (238) comprises reference absorption related wavelength-dependent data corresponding to one or more of ethanol, acetaldehyde, ethylacetate, methanol, acetone, ethylene, CO2, O2, and ethane. 21. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the data processing operation comprises aligning of the absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236) relative to one or more reference wavelength- dependent data (238) by wavelength-shifting of the absorption related wavelength- dependent data (236). 22. The storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the data processing operation comprises a data processing algorithm. 23. An arrangement comprising (i) a storage space (100) for produce (110) and (ii) the storage control system (10) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the storage control system (10) is configured to control a storage space parameter of the storage space (100). 24. A method for controlling a storage space parameter of a storage space (100) for produce (110), wherein the method comprises (i) reducing the water vapor content in a storage space gas sample, (ii) passing supercontinuum light source radiation through the storage space gas sample, (iii) sensing light passed through the storage space gas sample, (iv) providing a sensor signal (235), wherein the sensor signal (235) comprises absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236), (v) executing a data processing operation to provide a processed sensor signal (237), wherein the data processing operation comprises aligning of the absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236) relative to one or more reference wavelength-dependent data (238), and (v) controlling a storage space parameter dependent on the processed sensor signal (237). 25. The method according to claim 24, wherein the method is executed using the storage control system (10) according to any of claims 1-22. 26. The method according to any one of the preceding claims 24-25, wherein the data processing operation comprises aligning of the absorption related wavelength- dependent data (236) relative to one or more reference wavelength-dependent data (238) by wavelength-shifting of the absorption related wavelength-dependent data (236). 27. The method according to any one of the preceding claims 24-25, wherein the data processing operation comprises a data processing algorithm.
